name: CLI framework audit
description: CLI framework audit (Feb 2025): Mulch uses Commander v14 + chalk (reference implementation). Seeds, Canopy, Overstory all use custom manual argv parsing with switch statements and local getFlag/hasFlag helpers. All use raw ANSI codes for colors except Mulch which uses chalk. All support --json globally. All store data as JSONL with advisory file locks and atomic writes.

name: CLI command overlap map
description: Shared commands across tools: sync (all 4), prime (all 4), doctor (mulch/seeds/overstory), onboard (mulch/seeds/canopy), status/stats (mulch=status, seeds=stats, overstory=status, canopy=stats). All have --json on every command. Seeds/Canopy/Overstory run TypeScript directly via Bun (no build step). Mulch compiles to dist/ via tsc (Node/npm). Overstory is the only tool with shell completions (bash/zsh/fish).

name: CLI output and error patterns
description: Mulch uses chalk.green for success (✔), chalk.red for errors, process.exitCode=1 (testability). Seeds uses raw ANSI with status icons, process.exit(1). Canopy uses raw ANSI with ExitError class, process.exit(1). Overstory has richest error system (OverstoryError hierarchy with codes: CONFIG_ERROR, AGENT_ERROR, etc.), uses process.exit(1). JSON error shape is { success: false, command, error } in Seeds/Mulch; Overstory adds error code field.

title: CLI alignment strategy
rationale: Mulch is the reference implementation for CLI UX. All tools will adopt Commander for parsing and chalk for output. Seeds and Canopy get big-bang rewrites of CLI layer (business logic untouched). Overstory gets incremental migration due to its 30+ commands. No shared library — each tool is standalone; they just follow the same conventions. Tracked in root .seeds/ as os-eco-ff85 epic.

name: Tool versions and runtimes
description: Versions (Feb 2025): Mulch v0.5.0 (npm, Node >=18, tsc build, published as mulch-cli). Seeds v0.2.0 (Bun >=1.0, runs .ts directly, bin: sd). Canopy v0.1.2 (Bun >=1.0, runs .ts directly, bin: cn). Overstory v0.6.0 (Bun >=1.0, runs .ts directly, bin: overstory). All use Biome for linting except Mulch (tsc --noEmit only). All have scripts/version-bump.ts. VERSION const stored in entry point + package.json.

content: Cross-repo issue propagation: ecosystem-level issues live in os-eco/.seeds/. Mirror standalone copies into each sub-repo's .seeds/ tracker with no upstream references — sub-repos operate in isolation. Use os-eco issues for planning/tracking the big picture; sub-repo issues are what agents actually pick up and work on.

name: Multi-repo wave orchestration
description: Overstory+Sapling integration: two coordinators dispatched in parallel, each managing 3-4 waves sequentially. 44 min wall clock for 11 issues across 2 repos, 9 leads, 14 builders. Wave-based dispatch with dependency tracking in seeds blockers works well — coordinators autonomously sequence waves without orchestrator intervention.
Overstory coordinator stalls after ~20 min when watchdog is not running. Stops polling mail after first response cycle. Required 3 restarts for a 3-issue batch.
resolution: Restart coordinator with narrower scope (1-2 tasks per restart). Investigate watchdog keepalive and coordinator mail polling loop.

name: Coordinator watchdog critical for stability
description: Multi-repo wave orchestration: sapling coordinator (watchdog running) completed 5 issues in ~37 min, no stalls. Overstory coordinator (watchdog not running) stalled 3 times over ~80 min for 3 issues. Always verify watchdog is running after coordinator start.

Retrospective assumed single coordinator session because overstory events DB uses one session ID for all coordinators. Each task group actually had its own coordinator. Per-coordinator tracking is missing — can't attribute cost, duration, or failures to individual coordinators.
resolution: File overstory issue to give each coordinator its own session ID in the events DB. Update session list/show to surface per-coordinator data.

name: v1-audit-swarm-timing
description: V1_DONE.md audit swarm (2026-03-07): 6 repos dispatched. Seeds fastest (~12min). Mulch/canopy completed without sending result mail — check coordinator output as fallback after 30min. Sapling needed 4 builder attempts (~33min). Average ~20min per repo. All coordinators must cd into sub-repo to start (no --project flag).

name: parallel-two-repo-bugfix
description: Parallel two-repo bug fix swarm (seeds + mulch isInsideWorktree submodule false positive, 2026-04-03): dispatched identical-scope fix to both coordinators simultaneously. Seeds completed in ~8min, mulch in ~12min. Both used single lead + single builder topology. Total wall-clock ~13min for both repos.

title: Greenhouse archived 2026-05; warren absorbed the autonomous-loop role
rationale: Greenhouse (autonomous development daemon: GitHub poll -> ingest -> dispatch orchestrator -> open PR) was archived on GitHub and removed from the local os-eco workspace on 2026-05-17. Warren's control plane has absorbed the autonomous-loop role: it polls GitHub for triaged issues, dispatches runs through its own UI/API, and runs each agent inside an embedded burrow sandbox. Keeping a separate daemon was redundant once warren grew a label-watching ingester and a unified dispatch path; collapsing them removes one IPC boundary, one deploy target, and one tool to audit. The greenhouse GitHub repo remains readable (archived, not deleted); the npm package @os-eco/greenhouse-cli was never published. Per-tool branding/audit rows, historical seeds issues, and mulch records referencing greenhouse were preserved with explicit archival markers rather than scrubbed, so the ecosystem's history of having had a separate daemon stays intact.

content: os-eco META repo governance scaffolding (AGENTS.md, .github/dependabot.yml, PR template, ISSUE_TEMPLATE/) is ported from templates/l5-toolkit/github/ but ADAPTED for the no-package.json meta-repo: dependabot drops the npm ecosystem (keeps only github-actions weekly + cooldown), PR template Test plan swaps bun lint/typecheck/test for git-status-clean + sub-repo-scope checks, and bug_report.yml's version field becomes optional 'Affected sub-repo / version'. Commits here must stay root-scoped — never include sub-repo paths.

Root .gitignore appends env/IDE/OS/build-artifact baseline (.env, .env.*, !.env.example, .idea/, .vscode/, .DS_Store, node_modules/, dist/, coverage/, test-results/, *.log) below the sub-repo ignore lines — NEVER replace the sub-repo lines or the root commit recursively tracks every sub-repo working tree. .github/labels.yml (priority/P0-P3, kind/*, area/*, status/*) and .github/workflows/sync-labels.yml are copied VERBATIM from templates/l5-toolkit/github (VAL-CROSS-001 requires diff-equivalence between toolkit and consumers). sync-labels uses crazy-max/ghaction-github-labeler@v5 with skip-delete:true (additive), dry-run on PRs, full sync on push to main, checkout@v6. Closes VAL-OS-ECO-005,006.

Hand-authored sections in os-eco root CLAUDE.md must go ABOVE the auto-managed <!-- mulch:start -->/<!-- seeds:start -->/<!-- canopy:start --> blocks (e.g. between Scripts and Conventions); ml/sd/cn prime overwrite content inside those blocks. README repo-structure tree links to templates/l5-toolkit/ for L5 readiness kit discovery.

title: os-eco META milestone sealed (VAL-OS-ECO-FINAL)
rationale: Final milestone of the L5 uplift mission: this child is a verification+handoff seal, not feature work. All governance landed in children 1-3, so the seal only confirms presence/tracking and the toolkit-equivalence invariant before requesting the user's /readiness-report.
